hypothesis: Deploying a swarm of LLM agents with systematically varied model temperatures to explore the same atomic layer processing reactor simulation in parallel, with a meta-agent synthesizing their collective findings, will yield significantly greater coverage of the experimental space and discovery of rare chemical interactions (such as passivation and co-dosing effects) compared to sequential single-agent runs, because higher-temperature agents will escape local minima through stochastic path diversification while lower-temperature agents consolidate and verify discovered phenomena.
